mood.label: CAUTIOUSLY-RISK-ON
mood.summary: NVDA reports AMC tonight and FOMC May minutes hit 14:00 ET — Stage-2 backdrop intact (VIX 17.82, F&G 61 Greed) but the single most important variable today is NVDA Q1 print + guide. Sit on hands until the print clears or only take A+ EP setups with hard LOD stops.

indicesNarrative: Closed-firm Mon/Tue/Wed-eve into NVDA night — SPY/QQQ both green on May 19, VIX at 17.82 reflects complacency rather than fear. Futures point modestly higher pre-mkt but the tape is hostage to two events: NVDA AMC and FOMC minutes 14:00 ET. Index trend intact; no edge in fresh longs before the print.

sectorsThemes.name: Semiconductors
sectorsThemes.note: SMH closed $543.96 (May 19) — leadership group; ALAB/MRVL/ARM/CRDO all multi-percent up

sectorsNarrative: Surface rotation on May 19 was defensives-up / cyclicals-down (XLV +1.10% leading; XLF/XLI/XLY all -1%+). Under that read, semis kept printing fresh closing strength (ALAB +13%, CRDO +8%, MRVL +4%) — the leadership group is unchanged even as broad sectors mixed. The whole picture re-rates tonight on NVDA.
industryNarrative: Semiconductors are the only industry with multiple A-quality closes on the day and clear money flow; outside semis the tape is either modest-up (recreational, biotech selective) or red. Concentrated leadership = concentrated event risk: one NVDA print can re-rate the entire group up or down.

standout.thesis: No standout today — every quality setup is either extended or hostage to NVDA's print. The disciplined play is no new entries pre-AMC.
